facts.text: West Hempstead earned its name when the Long Island Rail Road built a station west of Hempstead in 1891; locals shortened 'West of Hempstead' to West Hempstead.
Three LIRR stations serve West Hempstead: West Hempstead, Hempstead Gardens, and Lakeview, connecting residents to the broader Long Island network.
Hall’s Pond Park, Nassau County’s 11‑acre green space, opened in 1961 and hosts summer concerts, playgrounds, and walking paths.
Echo Park’s indoor pool complex on Nassau Boulevard offers swimming, a diving tank, sauna, and fitness center for all Town of Hempstead residents.
West Hempstead enjoys easy access from the Southern State Parkway at Exit 17N, linking it to major Long Island thoroughfares.
Mom‑and‑pop shops, kosher eateries, and the historic Riesterer’s Bakery—founded in 1931—line Hempstead Avenue, while Hempstead Turnpike offers a broader retail mix.
